risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The extracted evidence only says the study was “randomized” and “double-blind” with “placebo group” and “olmesartan group,” but the method of sequence generation is not described. Comment: “Randomized” alone, without details such as computer generation, random numbers, blocks, or another explicit random method, is insufficient to confirm a truly random allocation sequence, and there is no positive evidence of a non-random method either.

Summary: The article states that ROADMAP was a "randomized, double-blind" trial, but it gives no description of how the allocation sequence was concealed from the person enrolling participants. The extracted evidence explicitly says the concealment mechanism is "not described" and the envelope features are unknown. Comment: Randomization alone does not establish allocation concealment; without an explicit central allocation, SNOSE, identical containers, or another concealment mechanism, the correct judgement is Unclear.

_evidence.note: The text describes the prior ROADMAP study as a "randomized, double-blind" trial and gives treatment groups, but provides no description of allocation concealment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The ROADMAP study was a randomized, double‐blind, prevention study" and "The ROADMAP study compared olmesartan medoxomil 40 mg o.d. versus placebo" Comment: The study explicitly states that it was double-blind and used placebo control, indicating participants and personnel were blinded to allocation during the intervention phase. The reported outcomes include both objective measures (e.g., microalbuminuria, eGFR, mortality) and some subjective/clinician-judged outcomes, but the stated successful double-blinding makes performance bias unlikely overall.

_evidence.blinding_quote: The ROADMAP study was a randomized, double‐blind, prevention study with the primary endpoint “time to first event of microalbuminuria.”1–2
_evidence.blinding_method: double-blind; olmesartan medoxomil 40 mg o.d. versus placebo

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Due to the observational character of the OFU only medical examinations and treatment according to the standard of medical care were documented. Therefore, in contrast to the ROADMAP study, microalbuminuria was detected according to different local standards and was not centrally assessed. CV and renal events reported by the investigators were not adjudicated by an independent endpoint‐monitoring committee." Comment: The outcome assessors were not centrally blinded/independently adjudicated, but all outcomes described here are objective or record-based (microalbuminuria testing, diabetic retinopathy diagnosis, cardiovascular/renal events, blood pressure, eGFR/laboratory parameters). For these outcomes, lack of blinded outcome assessment is unlikely to influence measurement materially, so overall detection bias is low.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: Due to the observational character of the OFU only medical examinations and treatment according to the standard of medical care were documented. Therefore, in contrast to the ROADMAP study, microalbuminuria was detected according to different local standards and was not centrally assessed. CV and renal events reported by the investigators were not adjudicated by an independent endpoint‐monitoring committee.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Out of 2198 eligible patients (placebo group 1104; olmesartan group: 1094) a total of 1758 patients (placebo group: [79.4%]; olmesartan group: [80.6%]) participated in the OFU." Comment: The analysed OFU cohorts were 877/1104 and 881/1094, so post-randomization loss was 227/1104 (20.6%) in placebo and 213/1094 (19.5%) in olmesartan, with only a 1.7 percentage-point difference between arms. This is moderate and balanced attrition, and the paper does not report outcome-related reasons or inappropriate missing-data handling that would suggest bias for the outcomes of interest.

per_arm_flow.reasons: "Out of 2198 eligible patients (placebo group 1104; olmesartan group: 1094) a total of 1758 patients (placebo group: [79.4%]; olmesartan group: [80.6%]) participated in the OFU."
"Out of 2198 eligible patients (placebo group 1104; olmesartan group: 1094) a total of 1758 patients (placebo group: [79.4%]; olmesartan group: [80.6%]) participated in the OFU."

_evidence.notes: Flow numbers are for participation in the observational follow-up cohort versus the original ROADMAP eligible patients; outcome analyses are reported on enrolled OFU participants, with some analyses restricted to subsets (eg, microalbuminuria tables n=436/426).
